# Plan — 2026-05-30

> Source: `.github/research/2026-05-30-research.md`
> Open auto-implement issues going in: 2 (`#530`, `#532`).
> Cap on new issues this cycle: 5.

## Summary

The 2026-05-30 research enumerated **six** new gaps + one deferred audit. Walking
the codebase, **five of seven are already shipped or already filed**, leaving
one true new task to file:

| Research item | Status now | Action |
|---|---|---|
| #1 Per-directory `AGENTS.md` injected as `<system-reminder>` | **Not implemented**: only project-root + global AGENTS.md are loaded once per session (`src/agent/system.ts:200-221`); no per-tool-call walk; no `systemReminders` channel on `ToolResult`. | **File new issue → 1 created.** |
| #2 DOCX + XLSX in `read` | **Already filed** as `#530`. | Skip. |
| #3 Plugin auto-load + `alexi plugin init` scaffolder | **Already filed** as `#532`. | Skip. |
| #4 Default `agent` field in user config | **Already implemented** — `getDefaultAgent`/`setDefaultAgent`/`clearDefaultAgent` in `src/config/userConfig.ts:187-212`. | Skip (drop). |
| #5 OSC-8 clickable URLs in TUI | **Already implemented** — `src/cli/tui/utils/hyperlink.ts` + `hyperlink.test.ts`. | Skip (drop). |
| #6 `SessionStart` hook → `reloadSkills: true` + `/reload-skills` command | **Already implemented** — `src/hooks/index.ts:83,112,555-562` + `src/command/reloadSkills.ts` + `src/skill/index.ts:452`. | Skip (drop). |
| Deferred POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell audit | **Already implemented** — `BUILTINS_DIRECTORY_MUTATORS` in `src/permission/shell-parser.ts:53` covers `cd`, `pushd`, `popd`, `chdir`; tests in `src/permission/__tests__/shell-parser.test.ts`. | Skip (drop). |

This is consistent with the research's executive summary: most of the
small wins from this cycle have already landed since the research was
written. Only Item #1 — the harder, higher-leverage behavioural change —
is still wide open.

## Plan entry — Per-directory AGENTS.md injection

### Files to create / modify

- **Modify** `src/agent/system.ts`
  - Export a new pure helper:
    ```ts
    export function instructionsForPath(
      absPath: string,
      workdir: string,
      seen: Set<string>,
    ): Array<{ path: string; content: string }>
    ```
  - It walks `path.dirname(absPath)` upward, stopping at (and **excluding** —
    that's already covered by `loadInstructionFiles`) `workdir`. At each step
    it looks for `AGENTS.md`. If present and its `realpathSync` form is not
    already in `seen`, it adds it to `seen` and pushes `{ path, content }`.
    Order: nearest-first.
  - Re-use `loadTextFile` and `safeRealpath` already in the file.
  - Refuse paths outside `workdir` (`path.relative(workdir, absPath)` starts
    with `..`) — return `[]`.

- **Modify** `src/tool/index.ts`
  - Extend the `metadata` typing comment in `ToolResult` to formally
    document the optional `systemReminders` channel:
    ```ts
    metadata?: {
      systemReminders?: Array<{ source: string; content: string }>;
      [k: string]: unknown;
    };
    ```
    (Keep the index signature for back-compat with anything already
    setting `metadata`.)

- **Modify** `src/tool/tools/read.ts`
  - After a successful read, call
    `instructionsForPath(absPath, context.workdir, sessionSeen)` (the seen
    set is per-session — see below) and, for any non-empty results, set
    `result.metadata.systemReminders = [{ source, content }, …]`.
  - The `sessionSeen` set lives on `ToolContext` (new optional field
    `agentsMdSeen?: Set<string>`) so multiple reads in one session don't
    re-emit the same file.

- **Modify** `src/tool/index.ts` (`ToolContext`)
  - Add `agentsMdSeen?: Set<string>` so the orchestrator can supply a
    per-session set.

- **Modify** `src/core/agenticChat.ts`
  - Allocate a per-execute `agentsMdSeen = new Set<string>()` near where
    `toolContext` is built (around `agenticChat.ts:493`) and pass it
    into `toolContext`.
  - In the post-tool-call block (`agenticChat.ts:633-639`), after pushing
    the `role: 'tool'` message, if
    `toolResult.metadata?.systemReminders?.length`, push **one** synthetic
    `role: 'user'` message containing the joined reminders wrapped in
    `<system-reminder source="…">…</system-reminder>` blocks. This matches
    the in-tree convention already used by hooks
    (see `agenticChat.ts:656-659`).

- **Modify** `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts`
  - Same per-execute `agentsMdSeen` allocation + pass-through (mirror the
    agenticChat change).

- **Add** `src/agent/system.test.ts` cases
  - Returns `[]` when no `AGENTS.md` exists between `workdir` and the file.
  - Returns reminders for `apps/api/AGENTS.md` when reading
    `apps/api/src/index.ts` from a `workdir` of the repo root.
  - De-dupes when the same file is read twice with a shared `seen` set.
  - Refuses paths outside `workdir`.
  - Symlinked `AGENTS.md` resolves via `realpath` and de-dupes against
    a sibling that points to the same target (mirrors the symlink-aware
    skill discovery pattern in `src/skill/index.ts:223-253`).

- **Add** `src/tool/tools/__tests__/read.agentsmd.test.ts`
  - Integration: read tool returns `metadata.systemReminders` populated
    when an intermediate `AGENTS.md` exists; empty (or absent) when not.
  - Honors the per-context `agentsMdSeen` so a second read of a sibling
    file does not re-emit the same reminder.

### Approach notes

- **Keep the walker pure** (no I/O outside `loadTextFile`/`existsSync`/
  `realpathSync` calls already available) — it's the same shape as
  `findGlobalAgentsMd` in `system.ts:169-189`.
- **Do not** alter the system-prompt assembly path in
  `loadInstructionFiles`. Per-directory injection is intentionally a
  per-tool-call event, not a session-start event, because that's how
  monorepo agents discover that `apps/api` has its own rules — by
  touching files there.
- **Do not** inject reminders for **the same file** twice in a session.
  Use `realpathSync`-based keys, identical to how
  `findGlobalAgentsMd` already de-dupes against the project AGENTS.md
  via `excludeResolved` (`system.ts:182-184`).
- **Wrap** each reminder in a single `<system-reminder source="…">` block
  to match the convention noted in `AGENTS.md` itself ("Tool results and
  user messages may include `<system-reminder>` tags"). One synthetic
  user message per tool call carrying all newly discovered reminders is
  cleaner than emitting one user message per file.
- **Performance**: cap walk depth at 32 to defend against pathological
  symlink layouts (matches the cycle-protection style in
  `src/skill/index.ts`).

### Tests to write

Listed above; total ~6-8 new test cases across two files. Coverage of
the new code path should comfortably clear the CI 40 % lines floor
because the helper is small and exercised by both the unit tests and the
read-tool integration test.

## Notes for the next research cycle

- The 2026-05-29 carry-over list and ~⅔ of the 2026-05-30 list closed
  during this cycle. Velocity is healthy; the next research pass should
  re-baseline against the v2.1.158+ window and the kilocode 7.4.x line
  rather than re-listing already-shipped items.
- Item #1 above will likely surface follow-on work: a `/memory list`
  enhancement to also surface the per-directory files an agent has
  picked up during a session. Good candidate for the next cycle.
- Re-evaluate next cycle: SAP-AI-Core auto-mode classifier availability,
  OTEL instrumentation demand, dynamic-workflows feasibility under
  single-binary CLI constraints. (Same set the research called out.)
